prompt: Use the $phased-delivery skill in read-only simulation mode. Do not contact Linear, GitHub, or mutate files. A fake issue DEN-456 is labeled large-refactor and asks for a multi-step orchestration change. Produce the workpad Phase Plan and explain how the current phase becomes one PR, how review routes to Rework, and how the next phase resumes after merge.

Use the $linear-workflow skill in read-only simulation mode. Do not contact Linear, GitHub, or mutate files. Given a fake issue DEN-123 in Todo asking for a small UI fix, describe the exact workflow you would follow from pickup through pushed PR and Human Review handoff. Include required pre-reads, validation gates, workpad behavior, PR handling, and handoff conditions.

Use the $workpad skill in read-only simulation mode. Do not contact Linear or mutate files. For fake issue DEN-123, produce the single persistent workpad update you would maintain after implementation is started, tests are pending, and one blocker is discovered.

Use the $push skill in read-only simulation mode. Do not run git push, gh, or contact GitHub. Given a completed fake branch for DEN-123, describe the safe publish and draft PR sequence, including checks before staging, PR body expectations, and what you would not do without confirmation.

Use the $debug skill in read-only simulation mode. Do not mutate files. A fake Symphony run for DEN-123 is stuck retrying with session abc123. Explain the concrete diagnostic sequence using issue/session identifiers, Symphony logs, Codex logs, and likely retry causes.
In one paragraph, explain what Linear is. Do not use any skill.
